summary: This notebook demonstrates a lightweight medical AI inference pipeline within the Kaggle environment. It downloads the Gemma 2B model via KerasNLP, processes simulated or auto-detected patient text inputs, applies a basic keyword-based safety filter, and exports results as structured JSON packets and audio reports. The code emphasizes practical deployment considerations like memory management, fallback simulation modes, and seamless integration with Kaggle's file system and secrets manager. Readers should understand how to efficiently run open-weight LLMs for text-based triage simulation without requiring full training pipelines.
competition_flow: Downloads a competition dataset via CLI, auto-detects CSV files, runs inference on patient text using a downloaded Gemma 2B model, applies a safety filter, and exports results as JSON and audio files.
data_reading: Uses os.walk('/kaggle/input') to locate CSV files and pd.read_csv() to load them.
Downloads competition data via !kaggle competitions download.
Falls back to hardcoded mock data if no CSV is detected.
data_processing: Strips the original prompt from model outputs and isolates the first response line.
Applies a simple keyword-based safety filter to flag high-risk outputs.
insights: Demonstrates efficient memory management on Kaggle GPUs using TF_GPU_ALLOCATOR and garbage collection.
Shows how to build robust fallback mechanisms that gracefully switch to simulation mode when datasets or models fail to load.
Illustrates practical prompt engineering and output parsing techniques for causal language models in a constrained environment.
